title: Game Startup and Initialization Flow
interaction_flow: ClientLauncher.setup() → UI.loadColors() [static: sets arc color palette]
ClientLauncher.setup() → new SpriteBatch(), new AssetManager(), new FileTree()
ClientLauncher.setup() → assets.load(new Vars()) [queues Vars as Loadable]

└─ Vars.init() → new Waves, EntityCollisions, World, Universe, BeControl, AsyncCore, MapEditor, Maps, WaveSpawner, BlockIndexer, Pathfinder, ControlPathfinder, FogControl, BaseRegistry, GlobalVars, GameState
└─ Vars.init() → mods.load() [scans mod directory], maps.load() [scans map directory]

└─ async: content.createBaseContent() → UnitCommand.loadAll, TeamEntries.load, Items.load, UnitStance.loadAll, StatusEffects.load, Liquids.load, Bullets.load, UnitTypes.load, Blocks.load, Loadouts.load, Weathers.load, Planets.load, SectorPresets.load, SerpuloTechTree.load, ErekirTechTree.load
└─ async: content.loadColors() [reads block_colors.png, sets Block.mapColor + squareSprite]
ClientLauncher.setup() → add(logic=new Logic()) — also queues Logic as Loadable
ClientLauncher.setup() → add(ui=new UI()) → UI() constructor calls Fonts.loadFonts()
└─ UI.loadSync(): Core.scene=new Scene(), Tex.load(), Icon.load(), Styles.load(), Tex.loadStyles(), Fonts.loadContentIcons(), sets up cursors
└─ async: content.init() → initialize(Content::init) for all content, initialize(Content::postInit), logicVars.init(), Events.fire(ContentInitEvent) → Vars creates emptyTile
└─ sync: content.load() → initialize(Content::loadIcon) for all content (inc. Floor), initialize(Content::load) for all content → Floor.load() loads texture regions
--- When assets.update() returns true (all assets loaded) ---

└─ UI.init() → creates all fragments (MenuFragment, HudFragment, ChatFragment, etc.) and all dialogs (JoinDialog, HostDialog, PausedDialog, SettingsMenuDialog, PlanetDialog, etc.), builds scene groups
ClientLauncher.update() → (deferred via app.post x4) finishLaunch() [deletes launchid.dat]
key_relationships: DesktopLauncher extends ClientLauncher extends ApplicationCore implements Platform
ClientLauncher contains AssetManager (drives all async loading)
Vars implements Loadable — loaded by AssetManager; Vars.init() is the core bootstrap
Vars.init() creates ContentLoader (stored as Vars.content static field)
ContentLoader.createBaseContent() is called async before mods; createModContent() sync after scripts
ContentLoader.init() fires ContentInitEvent which completes Vars.emptyTile setup
ContentLoader.load() calls load() on every Content instance including Floor (texture regions)
UI implements ApplicationListener + Loadable — loadSync sets up scene; init() builds all dialogs/fragments
All modules (Logic, Control, Renderer, UI, NetServer, NetClient) added via ClientLauncher.add() which also loads them as Loadable
ClientLoadEvent fires after all modules init(), marking game ready
loading_phases: Phase 2 (setup): AssetManager queuing — Vars, fonts, atlas, sounds, contentcreate, module Loadables, contentinit, baseparts
Phase 3 (per-frame async): assets.update() processes queued loads; LoadRenderer draws progress
Phase 4 (post-load sync): all module .init() called, ClientLoadEvent fired, finishLaunch()
important_notes: Floor is a Block subclass; its load() (loading texture regions from atlas) is invoked during the 'contentinit' loadRun sync phase via ContentLoader.load() → initialize(Content::load). The AssetManager controls the ordering of all loading phases — each loadRun has an async part (worker thread) and sync part (main thread after async). Vars.init() is NOT called directly from setup() — it runs asynchronously as Vars implements Loadable and is submitted to AssetManager. Steam integration is conditionally initialized in DesktopLauncher constructor before SDL starts.
